summary: Strong Help‑Slip‑Franklin run, powerful Black Peter close
blurb: The second set builds momentum with a seamless Help On The Way → Slipknot → Franklin’s Tower sequence and an explosive Black Peter jam that leads into Around & Around. Jerry delivers heartfelt solos on Masterpiece and So Many Roads despite occasional vocal slips, while Vince adds texture in the Space/Watchtower segment.
review: June 22, 1995 at Albany’s Knickerbocker Arena captured a night of high‑water moments mixed with a few rough edges. The first set wandered a bit – Touch of Grey and Walkin’ Blues felt serviceable but uneven – yet the band truly found its stride after the intermission. A magical Help On The Way → Slipknot → Franklin’s Tower segue carried the show into a soaring second half, punctuated by Vince Welnick’s lush keyboards that even stepped in when Jerry’s voice wavered on “Franklin’s Tower.” The highlight was an emphatic Black Peter jam that surged through Space and All Along The Watchtower before spilling out into a rollicking Around & Around, sending the crowd home with a grin. Jerry’s guitar work on “When I Paint My Masterpiece” and “So Many Roads” remained soulful, even as his vocals stumbled on “Touch of Grey.” Bob Weir anchored the band, especially on Black Peter, while Phil Lesh kept the low end solid throughout. Overall, despite a few off‑notes, the performance shines as one of the stronger shows from their final tour.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings (audience and fan‑hype) are clearly positive, praising the Help/Slip/Franklin sequence, Black Peter jam and overall emotional impact. One recording is mixed, noting occasional vocal/guitar slips but still highlighting strong moments. The fourth review is negative with no detail. The net sentiment leans modestly positive, warranting a +0.7 adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Good‑quality source with clear, balanced sound that captures the key highlights while minimizing dropouts; it presents the strongest overall listening experience despite occasional performance slips.

key_highlights: Seamless Help On The Way → Slipknot → Franklin’s Tower sequence
Explosive Black Peter jam leading into Around & Around
Emotional solos on When I Paint My Masterpiece and So Many Roads
Vince Welnick’s rich keyboard textures during Space/Watchtower

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers heartfelt solos on Masterpiece and So Many Roads, but his vocals wobble on Franklin’s Tower and Touch of Grey; overall an emotionally charged but occasionally uneven performance.
band_performance.Bob: Weir shines on Black Peter, providing solid rhythm throughout and adding extra spark in the second set.
band_performance.Brent: Vince steps in when Jerry falters—cutting him off on Samba—and supplies lush texture, especially during the Space/Watchtower jam.
